stem: The function $p$ is defined by $p(x) = x^2 - 9$. Which of the following gives all values of $x$ for which $p(x) = 0$?
explanation:
Set $p(x) = 0$ and solve:

$$x^2 - 9 = 0$$
$$x^2 = 9$$
$$x = \pm\sqrt{9} = \pm 3$$

So the solutions are $x = -3$ and $x = 3$.

Alternatively, factor the difference of squares: $x^2 - 9 = (x-3)(x+3) = 0$, giving $x = 3$ or $x = -3$.

distractorRationale.A: A student who takes only the positive square root of $9$ and ignores the negative solution reports $x = 3$ only. This error omits the negative root when solving $x^2 = 9$.
distractorRationale.B: This is the correct answer. Solving $x^2 - 9 = 0$ gives $x^2 = 9$, so $x = \pm 3$.
distractorRationale.C: A student who skips taking the square root entirely and sets $x = \pm 9$ directly from $x^2 = 9$ reports $x = -9$ and $x = 9$. This error omits the square root operation.
distractorRationale.D: A student who takes the square root of the coefficient $9$ incorrectly as $\sqrt{3}$ (perhaps misreading $9$ as $3$ under a radical) reports $x = \pm\sqrt{3}$. This error computes $\sqrt{9}$ as $\sqrt{3}$ instead of $3$.
